sourceNote: Community-aggregated advertised pull rates for a standard 24-pack One Piece booster box, from TCGTalk's box-pull-rate guide (2026): Leader ~1 per 2 packs (~12/box), Super Rare ~1 per 3 packs (~8/box), Parallel/Alternate Art ~2/box (~1 per 12 packs), Secret Rare ~1/box (~1 per 24 packs), Special Rare ~1 per case (~1 per 144 packs), Manga Rare ~1 per 2-6 cases (~1 in 576-1,728 packs). Bandai does not publish official odds, and this source discloses no sample size, so this table is capped at LOW confidence by design. The uniform-within-tier assumption is especially rough for One Piece: the Alternate Art tier spans sub-$5 commons to $100+ chase cards, so per-card odds within a tier are indicative only. Prices blend Scrydex (TCGplayer market) and PriceCharting (eBay-sold); cards without a matched price are excluded from their tier average, never counted as $0. The Special (SP) tier is deliberately NOT modelled in EV: SP cards are priced by a single source (TCGplayer market via Scrydex) with no second source to cross-check, and thin-market figures proved unreliable (an SP leader was listed at ~$6,969 against a real market of ~$200), so counting them would distort box EV. They remain in the catalog.
